mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month. A positive quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 consistently yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and reduce recession risk, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and expanding consumer credit indicate accommodative financial conditions that compress discount rates and stimulate demand, boosting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low rates and rising credit fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing demand signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion for industrial firms, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO momentum remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat during periods of rising industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms underlying economic momentum, reducing equity risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months consistently yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 1.0 million annualized units reflects strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not statistically higher during months where HOUST exceeds 1000 compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling consumer credit indicates a supply-led growth phase that typically precedes equity drawdowns due to margin compression and demand exhaustion, predicting negative returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations combined with a rising federal funds rate signal an aggressive tightening cycle that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat in more than 60% of instances where both inflation and rates rise simultaneously.
